slides.title: Feelings Vocabulary
slides.content:
Music can make us feel many different things! Let's learn some feelings words:

😄 excited — very happy and full of energy
😴 sleepy — feeling tired, ready for bed
😰 nervous — a bit worried or scared
😢 unhappy — sad, not feeling good
😌 peaceful — calm and relaxed
slides.tip: Feelings words describe how we feel inside. Ask yourself: How does this song make me feel?

slides.title: More Feelings Words
slides.content:
Here are more words about how music makes us feel:

😊 smile — when your mouth goes up because you are happy
😢 cry — when tears come from your eyes because you are sad
🥇 proud — feeling good about something you did well
✨ peace — a feeling of calm, no worries
👍 well — feeling good and healthy
slides.tip: Proud = orgulloso/a. You feel proud when you do something great!

slides.title: Action Words with Music
slides.content:
Some important verbs for talking about music:

🎤 live — to perform music in front of people (a live concert)
💡 light — not heavy; also something that shines (the light in the room)

Example: The band plays live music. The light makes me feel happy.

slides.title: Music at School
slides.html:
Today is the school concert! Emma feels excited because she is going to sing live on stage.

Her friend Tom feels a bit nervous. He is playing the drums for the first time!

The first song is slow and peaceful. Some people smile and close their eyes. The second song is fast and fun. Everyone feels excited and claps their hands!

At the end, Tom feels proud. He didn't make any mistakes! Emma gives him a big smile.

slides.title: Prepositions of Time: AT
slides.content:
We use at with exact times:

⏰ at 3 o'clock
⏰ at half past five
⏰ at 10:15

Example: The concert starts at 6 o'clock.
slides.tip: AT = exact time. Think of pointing AT the clock!

slides.title: Prepositions of Time: ON
slides.content:
We use on with days of the week:

📅 on Monday
📅 on Friday
📅 on Saturday morning

Example: We have music class on Wednesday.
slides.tip: ON = days. Think of a day ON the calendar!

slides.title: Prepositions of Time: IN
slides.content:
We use in with months and parts of the day:

📆 in January / in March / in December
🌅 in the morning
☀️ in the afternoon
🌙 in the evening

Example: The school play is in June. I practise piano in the afternoon.
slides.tip: IN = months and parts of the day. We are IN a big period of time!

slides.title: Choose: at, on, or in?
slides.problem:
Fill in the gaps with at, on or in:

1. The concert is _____ Tuesday.
2. I wake up _____ 7 o'clock.
3. We go on holiday _____ July.
4. I read books _____ the evening.
steps: 1. Tuesday is a day --> ON Tuesday
2. 7 o'clock is an exact time --> AT 7 o'clock
3. July is a month --> IN July
4. The evening is a part of the day --> IN the evening

slides.title: A Special Day
slides.html:
On Saturday, Mia went to a live concert with her family. The concert started at 5 o'clock in the afternoon.

The first song was slow and peaceful. Mia felt calm. Then the band played a fast song. Mia felt excited and wanted to dance!

A sad song made her mum cry a little. But then a happy song made everyone smile again. In the evening, they drove home. Mia felt sleepy but very happy.

slides.title: Feelings Match
slides.problem:
Match each sentence with the correct feeling:

1. I have a test tomorrow. I feel _____.
2. It's my birthday! I feel _____.
3. I did my best in the race. I feel _____.
4. The lullaby is soft. I feel _____.
steps: 1. A test makes you worried --> nervous
2. A birthday makes you very happy --> excited
3. Doing your best makes you feel good about yourself --> proud
4. A lullaby is calm and gentle --> sleepy

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 😊 Feelings: excited, sleepy, nervous, peaceful, unhappy, proud
😄 smile = happy face; cry = tears when sad
📆 IN + month or part of day: in June, in the morning
🎵 Music can make us feel many different things!
